case: 24x24 apple -- reference import vs freehand-from-imagination (INDEPENDENT BLIND JUDGE PANEL)
step_toggled: starting from a reference via live_import_reference vs inventing the organic shape from scratch
also_verifies_live: live_import_reference (base path) live E2E: decoded a 48x48 apple PNG, content-aware DOMINANT downscale to 24x24, CIELAB-snapped to a 6-colour palette, drew 231 px, 6 distinct colours (all on-palette).
method: WITHOUT: drew a 24x24 apple freehand from imagination, no reference = cand_noref. WITH: generated an organic 48x48 apple reference (smooth radial gradient) and live_import_reference'd it to 24x24 snapped to the same 6-colour palette = cand_ref. Both rendered CLEAN; scored BLIND by an independent 3-judge panel (scrambled labels, hypothesis withheld) on rules/06.
judge_observations_blind: All 3 judges picked the reference-grounded apple. Unprompted, they diagnosed the freehand's exact weaknesses -- 'three flat horizontal bands... textbook banding... no directional roundness... near-perfect circle... unnatural angular skirt' -- and praised the reference one's 'directional light... organic slightly-lobed silhouette... believable top dimple... value steps follow the curvature'.
verdict: Reference grounding produced a markedly better organic sprite: 3/3 blind judges, +4.0 overall, driven by form/light (+5.0) and proportions (+3.67). This directly targets the research's #1 hardest problem (inventing organic shapes from text) and is the largest measured cross-path delta so far. It also live-verifies live_import_reference end-to-end.
caveats: The reference was a synthetic organic image; a photo / AI-render reference would be the natural extension.
The 'with' is the raw import result (downscale+snap); tracing/cleaning over it would refine further.
Registered live binary predates regrid/auto_colors (base import only) -- those two SPEC-006 Phase-2 features remain unverified live until a rebuild.
